verses.latin_vulgate: Judas Jesu Christi servus, frater autem Jacobi, his qui sunt in Deo Patre dilectis, et Christo Jesu conservatis, et vocatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰουδας ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ δοῦλος, ἀδελφὸς δὲ ἰακώβου, τοῖς ἐν θεῷ πατρὶ ἠγιασμένοις, καὶ ἰησοῦ χριστῷ τετηρημένοις κλητοῖς
verses.king_james: Jude, the servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to them that are sanctified by God the Father, and preserved in Jesus Christ, *and* called:

verses.latin_vulgate: Misericordia vobis, et pax, et caritas adimpleatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεος ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη καὶ ἀγάπη πληθυνθείη
verses.king_james: Mercy unto you, and peace, and love, be multiplied.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissimi, omnem sollicitudinem faciens scribendi vobis de communi vestra salute, necesse habui scribere vobis: deprecans supercertari semel traditæ sanctis fidei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητοὶ, πᾶσαν σπουδὴν ποιούμενος γράφειν ὑμῖν περὶ τῆς κοινῆς σωτηρίας ἀνάγκην ἔσχον γράψαι ὑμῖν, παρακαλῶν ἐπαγωνίζεσθαι τῇ ἅπαξ παραδοθείσῃ τοῖς ἁγίοις πίστει
verses.king_james: Beloved, when I gave all diligence to write unto you of the common salvation, it was needful for me to write unto you, and exhort *you* that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subintroierunt enim quidam homines (qui olim præscripti sunt in hoc judicium) impii, Dei nostri gratiam transferentes in luxuriam, et solum Dominatorem, et Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum negantes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρεισέδυσαν γάρ τινες ἄνθρωποι οἱ πάλαι προγεγραμμένοι εἰς τοῦτο τὸ κρῖμα, ἀσεβεῖς, τὴν τοῦ θεοῦ ἡμῶν χάριν μετατιθέντες εἰς ἀσέλγειαν, καὶ τὸν μόνον δεσπότην θεὸν, καὶ κύριον ἡμῶν ἰησοῦν χριστὸν ἀρνούμενοι
verses.king_james: For there are certain men crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciviousness, and denying the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Commonere autem vos volo, scientes semel omnia, quoniam Jesus populum de terra Ægypti salvans, secundo eos, qui non crediderunt, perdidit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπομνῆσαι δὲ ὑμᾶς βούλομαι, εἰδότας ὑμᾶς ἅπαξ τοῦτο, ὅτι ὁ κύριος λαὸν ἐκ γῆς αἰγύπτου σώσας, τὸ δεύτερον τοὺς μὴ πιστεύσαντας ἀπώλεσεν
verses.king_james: I will therefore put you in remembrance, though ye once knew this, how that the Lord, having saved the people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed them that believed not.

verses.latin_vulgate: angelos vero, qui non servaverunt suum principatum, sed dereliquerunt suum domicilium, in judicium magni diei, vinculis æternis sub caligine reservavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγγέλους τε τοὺς μὴ τηρήσαντας τὴν ἑαυτῶν ἀρχὴν, ἀλλὰ ἀπολιπόντας τὸ ἴδιον οἰκητήριον, εἰς κρίσιν μεγάλης ἡμέρας, δεσμοῖς ἀϊδίοις ὑπὸ ζόφον τετήρηκεν
verses.king_james: And the angels which kept not their first estate, but left their own habitation, he hath reserved in everlasting chains under darkness unto the judgment of the great day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut Sodoma, et Gomorrha, et finitimæ civitates simili modo exfornicatæ, et abeuntes post carnem alteram, factæ sunt exemplum, ignis æterni pœnam sustinentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς σόδομα καὶ γόμοῤῥα, καὶ αἱ περὶ αὐτὰς πόλεις τὸν ὅμοιον τούτοις τρόπον ἐκπορνεύσασαι, καὶ ἀπελθοῦσαι ὀπίσω σαρκὸς ἑτέρας, πρόκεινται δεῖγμα, πυρὸς αἰωνίου δίκην ὑπέχουσαι
verses.king_james: Even as Sodom and Gomorrha, and the cities about them in like manner, giving themselves over to fornication, and going after strange flesh, are set forth for an example, suffering the vengeance of eternal fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similiter et hi carnem quidem maculant, dominationem autem spernunt, majestatem autem blasphemant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοίως μέντοι καὶ οὗτοι ἐνυπνιαζόμενοι σάρκα μὲν μιαίνουσιν, κυριότητα δὲ ἀθετοῦσι, δόξας δὲ βλασφημοῦσιν
verses.king_james: Likewise also these *filthy* dreamers defile the flesh, despise dominion, and speak evil of dignities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum Michaël Archangelus cum diabolo disputans altercaretur de Moysi corpore, non est ausus judicium inferre blasphemiæ: sed dixit: Imperet tibi Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ μιχαὴλ ὁ ἀρχάγγελος ὅτε τῷ διαβόλῳ διακρινόμενος διελέγετο περὶ τοῦ μωσέως σώματος, οὐκ ἐτόλμησεν κρίσιν ἐπενεγκεῖν βλασφημίας, ἀλλ᾽ εἶπεν, ἐπιτιμήσαι σοι κύριος
verses.king_james: Yet Michael the archangel, when contending with the devil he disputed about the body of Moses, durst not bring against him a railing accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi autem quæcumque quidem ignorant, blasphemant: quæcumque autem naturaliter, tamquam muta animalia, norunt, in his corrumpuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοι δὲ ὅσα μὲν οὐκ οἴδασιν, βλασφημοῦσιν ὅσα δὲ φυσικῶς ὡς τὰ ἄλογα ζῶα ἐπίστανται, ἐν τούτοις φθείρονται
verses.king_james: But these speak evil of those things which they know not: but what they know naturally, as brute beasts, in those things they corrupt themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ illis, quia in via Cain abierunt, et errore Balaam mercede effusi sunt, et in contradictione Core perierunt!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ αὐτοῖς, ὅτι τῇ ὁδῷ τοῦ κάϊν ἐπορεύθησαν, καὶ τῇ πλάνῃ τοῦ βαλαὰμ μισθοῦ ἐξεχύθησαν, καὶ τῇ ἀντιλογίᾳ τοῦ κορὲ ἀπώλοντο
verses.king_james: Woe unto them! for they have gone in the way of Cain, and ran greedily after the error of Balaam for reward, and perished in the gainsaying of Core.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi sunt in epulis suis maculæ, convivantes sine timore, semetipsos pascentes, nubes sine aqua, quæ a ventis circumferentur, arbores autumnales, infructuosæ, bis mortuæ, eradicatæ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοί εἰσιν ἐν ταῖς ἀγάπαις ὑμῶν, σπιλάδες, συνευωχούμενοι, ἀφόβως ἑαυτοὺς ποιμαίνοντες νεφέλαι ἄνυδροι, ὑπὸ ἀνέμων περιφερόμεναι, δένδρα φθινοπωρινὰ, ἄκαρπα, δὶς ἀποθανόντα, ἐκριζωθέντα
verses.king_james: These are spots in your feasts of charity, when they feast with you, feeding themselves without fear: clouds *they are* without water, carried about of winds; trees whose fruit withereth, without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots;

verses.latin_vulgate: fluctus feri maris, despumantes suas confusiones, sidera errantia: quibus procella tenebrarum servata est in æternum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κύματα ἄγρια θαλάσσης, ἐπαφρίζοντα τὰς ἑαυτῶν αἰσχύνας, ἀστέρες πλανῆται, οἷς ὁ ζόφος τοῦ σκότους εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τετήρηται
verses.king_james: Raging waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame; wandering stars, to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prophetavit autem et de his septimus ab Adam Enoch, dicens: Ecce venit Dominus in sanctis millibus suis
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προεφήτευσεν δὲ καὶ τούτοις ἕβδομος ἀπὸ ἀδὰμ ἑνὼχ, λέγων, ἰδοὺ ἦλθε κύριος ἐν μυριάσιν ἁγίαις αὑτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Enoch also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord cometh with ten thousands of his saints,

verses.latin_vulgate: facere judicium contra omnes, et arguere omnes impios de omnibus operibus impietatis eorum, quibus impie egerunt, et de omnibus duris, quæ locuti sunt contra Deum peccatores impii.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιῆσαι κρίσιν κατὰ πάντων, καὶ ἐξελέγξαι πάντας τοὺς ἀσεβεῖς αὐτῶν, περὶ πάντων τῶν ἔργων ἀσεβείας αὑτῶν ὧν ἠσέβησαν, καὶ περὶ πάντων τῶν σκληρῶν ὧν ἐλάλησαν κατ᾽ αὐτοῦ ἁμαρτωλοὶ ἀσεβεῖς
verses.king_james: To execute judgment upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard *speeches* which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi sunt murmuratores querulosi, secundum desideria sua ambulantes, et os eorum loquitur superba, mirantes personas quæstus causa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοί εἰσιν γογγυσταὶ, μεμψίμοιροι, κατὰ τὰς ἐπιθυμίας αὑτῶν πορευόμενοι, καὶ τὸ στόμα αὐτῶν λαλεῖ ὑπέρογκα, θαυμάζοντες πρόσωπα, ὠφελείας χάριν
verses.king_james: These are murmurers, complainers, walking after their own lusts; and their mouth speaketh great swelling *words*, having men's persons in admiration because of advantage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem carissimi, memores estote verborum, quæ prædicta sunt ab apostolis Domini nostri Jesu Christi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ ἀγαπητοὶ μνήσθητε τῶν ῥημάτων τῶν προειρημένων ὑπὸ τῶν ἀποστόλων τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: But, beloved, remember ye the words which were spoken before of the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui dicebant vobis, quoniam in novissimo tempore venient illusores, secundum desideria sua ambulantes in impietatibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἔλεγον ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐν ἐσχάτῳ χρόνῳ ἔσονται ἐμπαῖκται, κατὰ τὰς ἑαυτῶν ἐπιθυμίας πορευόμενοι τῶν ἀσεβειῶν
verses.king_james: How that they told you there should be mockers in the last time, who should walk after their own ungodly lusts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi sunt, qui segregant semetipsos, animales, Spiritum non habentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοί εἰσιν οἱ ἀποδιορίζοντες, ψυχικοὶ, πνεῦμα μὴ ἔχοντες
verses.king_james: These be they who separate themselves, sensual, having not the Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem carissimi superædificantes vosmetipsos sanctissimæ vestræ fidei, in Spiritu Sancto orantes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ, ἀγαπητοὶ, τῇ ἁγιωτάτῃ ὑμῶν πίστει ἐποικοδομοῦντες ἑαυτοὺς, ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ προσευχόμενοι
verses.king_james: But ye, beloved, building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Ghost,

verses.latin_vulgate: vosmetipsos in dilectione Dei servate, exspectantes misericordiam Domini nostri Jesu Christi in vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑαυτοὺς ἐν ἀγάπῃ θεοῦ τηρήσατε, προσδεχόμενοι τὸ ἔλεος τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ, εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: Keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hos quidem arguite judicatos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὓς μὲν ἐλεεῖτε διακρινόμενοι
verses.king_james: And of some have compassion, making a difference:

verses.latin_vulgate: illos vero salvate, de igne rapientes. Aliis autem miseremini in timore: odientes et eam, quæ carnalis est, maculatam tunicam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὓς δὲ ἐν φόβῳ σώζετε, ἐκ τοῦ πυρὸς ἁρπάζοντες, μισοῦντες καὶ τὸν ἀπὸ τῆς σαρκὸς ἐσπιλωμένον χιτῶνα
verses.king_james: And others save with fear, pulling *them* out of the fire; hating even the garment spotted by the flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ei autem qui potens est vos conservare sine peccato et constituere ante conspectum gloriæ suæ immaculatos in exsultatione in adventu Domini nostri Jesu Christi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ δυναμένῳ φυλάξαι αὐτούς ἀπταίστους, καὶ στῆσαι κατενώπιον τῆς δόξης αὑτοῦ ἀμώμους ἐν ἀγαλλιάσει
verses.king_james: Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present *you* faultless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy,

verses.latin_vulgate: soli Deo Salvatori nostro, per Jesum Christum Dominum nostrum, gloria et magnificentia, imperium et potestas ante omne sæculum, et nunc, et in omnia sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μόνῳ σοφῷ θεῷ σωτῆρι ἡμῶν δόξα καὶ μεγαλωσύνη, κράτος καὶ ἐξουσία, καὶ νῦν καὶ εἰς πάντας τοὺς αἰῶνας ἀμήν
verses.king_james: To the only wise God our Saviour, *be* glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and ever. Amen.
